title: Making static binaries fails
body:
I'm trying to make a static skopeo binary that will run on older Linux versions...

Running into:

```
yuvipanda@courage:~/code/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo$ make binary-local-static
go build -ldflags "-extldflags \"-static\" -X main.gitCommit=91606d49f2f7d17c226940e98b5f42500a4c3efb" -gcflags "" -tags " " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
# github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/cmd/skopeo
/home/yuvipanda/.local/go/pkg/tool/linux_amd64/link: running gcc failed: exit status 1
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lostree-1
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -ldevmapper
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -ldevmapper
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-glocalfileinfo.o): In function `lookup_gid_name':
(.text+0x11d7): warning: Using 'getgrgid_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-glocalvfs.o): In function `g_local_vfs_parse_name':
(.text+0x1e5): warning: Using 'getpwnam' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libglib-2.0.a(libglib_2_0_la-gutils.o): In function `g_get_user_database_entry':
(.text+0x26d): warning: Using 'getpwuid' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libglib-2.0.a(libglib_2_0_la-gutils.o): In function `g_get_user_database_entry':
(.text+0xdc): warning: Using 'getpwnam_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-glocalfileinfo.o): In function `lookup_uid_data':
(.text+0x1034): warning: Using 'getpwuid_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-699048108/000002.o: In function `_cgo_cb4050e05860_C2func_getaddrinfo':
/tmp/workdir/go/src/net/cgo-gcc-prolog:46: warning: Using 'getaddrinfo' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-gnetworkaddress.o): In function `g_network_address_parse':
(.text+0xcbb): warning: Using 'getservbyname' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/6/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-gnetworkaddress.o): In function `g_network_address_parse':
(.text+0xcd0): warning: Using 'endservent' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

Makefile:63: recipe for target 'binary-local-static' failed
make: *** [binary-local-static] Error 2
```

Note that `make binary-local` works, so I do have the libraries...

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Do you have _static versions_ of the libraries (`libdevmapper.a`, `libostree-1.a`) available as well?
I don't think so.  I am pretty sure using these on older versions is not likely to work anyways.

I only wanna extract a docker image into an oci bundle as non root anyway,
which shouldn't require these libraries...

I don't think I've specific static versions of these no...

On Jul 26, 2017 10:58 AM, "Daniel J Walsh" <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> I don't think so. I am pretty sure using these on older versions is not
> likely to work anyways.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/issues/385#issuecomment-318133556>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAB23q3wjpbTaNLFFSKgGlCpJj5eMAkWks5sR35UgaJpZM4OjYkR>
> .
>
What we need is BUILDTAGS for each storage type to be able to compile them out.

Yup that would be awesome!

On Jul 26, 2017 11:59 AM, "Daniel J Walsh" <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> What we need is BUILDTAGS for each storage type to be able to compile them
> out.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/issues/385#issuecomment-318150250>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAB23kwqfGhVRqth9AgcWWrxVDVwH94sks5sR4yggaJpZM4OjYkR>
> .
>

BUILDTAGS=containers_image_ostree_stub 
will remove ostree, but no way to get rid of devicemapper yet.
Not sure how you handle the getpw calls in glibc either.

Try building with This tag also.  
exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper

> Not sure how you handle the getpw calls in glibc either.

glibc just does not support static linking well enough anymore. It would probably be much smoother to create a container or a VM or that old distribution, and create a dynamically-linked binary on that; then we can rely on the glibc backward compatiblity guarantees.  (There could still be difficulties with the ostree and device-mapper libraries, so compiling those out might still be necessary.)
I hit the same issue on master. Seems that this is not yet resolved.
https://github.com/containers/image/pull/355 should, after it is merged, make it easy to get rid of containers-storage and the device-mapper dependency, but glibc’s NSS will continue to be an issue.
@runcom is there a fix for this? I need to build a static image so I can move it into a minimal Docker image at runtime.
@duglin have you tried to build a static skopeo?  What issues are you seeing?

@rhatdan yep - here's the output I see:
```
$ make binary-static
docker build  -f Dockerfile.build -t skopeobuildimage .
Sending build context to Docker daemon 44.58 MB
Step 1/4 : FROM ubuntu:17.10
 ---> 14107f6d2c97
Step 2/4 : RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y     golang     btrfs-tools     git-core     libdevmapper-dev     libgpgme11-dev     go-md2man     libglib2.0-dev     libostree-dev
 ---> Using cache
 ---> a8e933df3129
Step 3/4 : ENV GOPATH=/
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 06854cfb05d6
Step 4/4 : WORKDIR /src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 2ddf5717ca2b
Successfully built 2ddf5717ca2b
Successfully tagged skopeobuildimage:latest
docker run --rm --security-opt label:disable -v $(pwd):/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo \
	skopeobuildimage make binary-local-static  BUILDTAGS='btrfs_noversion  '
go build -ldflags "-extldflags \"-static\" -X main.gitCommit=ab2bc6e8d1705c643161f6e5b354482624dc2091" -gcflags "" -tags "btrfs_noversion  " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
# github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/cmd/skopeo
/usr/lib/go-1.8/pkg/tool/linux_amd64/link: running gcc failed: exit status 1
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lostree-1
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lostree-1
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -ldevmapper
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000009.o: In function `mygetgrouplist':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/os/user/getgrouplist_unix.go:15: warning: Using 'getgrouplist' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000009.o: In function `mygetgrgid_r':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/os/user/lookup_unix.go:38: warning: Using 'getgrgid_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000009.o: In function `mygetgrnam_r':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/os/user/lookup_unix.go:43: warning: Using 'getgrnam_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/7/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-glocalvfs.o): In function `g_local_vfs_parse_name':
(.text+0x17a): warning: Using 'getpwnam' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/7/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libglib-2.0.a(libglib_2_0_la-gutils.o): In function `g_get_user_database_entry':
(.text+0x267): warning: Using 'getpwuid' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000009.o: In function `mygetpwnam_r':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/os/user/lookup_unix.go:33: warning: Using 'getpwnam_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000009.o: In function `mygetpwuid_r':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/os/user/lookup_unix.go:28: warning: Using 'getpwuid_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/tmp/go-link-450359993/000004.o: In function `_cgo_cb4050e05860_C2func_getaddrinfo':
/build/golang-1.8-GaFns3/golang-1.8-1.8.3/src/net/cgo-gcc-prolog:46: warning: Using 'getaddrinfo' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
/usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/7/../../../x86_64-linux-gnu/libgio-2.0.a(libgio_2_0_la-gnetworkaddress.o): In function `g_network_address_parse':
(.text+0xcdb): warning: Using 'getservbyname' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
(.text+0xcf0): warning: Using 'endservent' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

Makefile:81: recipe for target 'binary-local-static' failed
make: *** [binary-local-static] Error 2
Makefile:72: recipe for target 'binary-static' failed
make: *** [binary-static] Error 2

```

Something like
```sh
make binary-local-static BUILDTAGS='containers_image_openpgp containers_image_ostree_stub containers_image_storage_stub'
```
works for me on Fedora, and shows how to disable the various components if the libraries are not available.

But, really, the glibc warning is still applicable, which makes the `binary-static` `make` target (building _in a container_, statically linking and expecting some other environment to match the glibc in the container) dangerous and unreliable.

I’m leaning towards just removing it. The only recommended build is a dynamically-linked one; people who want static linking would have to set up the _correct_ environment (in their own container, perhaps) and use `make binary-local-static`. Any objections?

I feel like I'm missing something. Shouldn't a docker build of the static exe be able to work no matter what my env is? Shouldn't the container used to do the build have everything pulled in (via the Dockerfile) so that it can find/link all libs and produce a truly stand-alone exe?

> Shouldn't a docker build of the static exe be able to work no matter what my env is? 

No, see https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/issues/385#issuecomment-318155892 .  User account lookup and the like are inherently and unavoidably dynamically linked.

> Shouldn't the container used to do the build have everything pulled in (via the Dockerfile) so that it can find/link all libs and produce a truly stand-alone exe?

As long as we maintain that `Makefile` target, sure. But if it can’t work the way it would be reasonably expected to (as you do), I’m leaning towards removing the target rather than overpromising.

Well, I just tried a variant of your cmd line:
`make binary-static BUILDTAGS='containers_image_openpgp containers_image_ostree_stub containers_image_storage_stub'`
and that seems to work great for me - so I'm good.  Thanks!
Ok I am going to close this issue then.
